coreIssue: This onboarding check-in is about whether a mid-level Backend Engineer has the right team operating map, not just codebase familiarity.
openingQuestion: What parts of ownership, incident handling, or architecture context still feel fuzzy enough that you might make the wrong call alone?
listenFor: whether he names specific people or channels he would use before making a design or incident decision
whether he gives a concrete recent example from week two instead of speaking only in generalities
if they describe ownership boundaries in ways that differ from how your team actually splits responsibility
avoid: do not mistake fast codebase ramp-up for clarity on team norms and decision paths
do not answer every uncertainty immediately before you see where his mental model is incomplete
goodOutcome: You and Riley have agreed what good mid-level Backend Engineer judgment looks like here for ownership boundaries and incident escalation, plus one concrete gap in onboarding support to fix now.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one real example of a recent architecture or incident decision on your team that shows who owns what, when to ask for context, and when to escalate.
